seoTitle: How to Change a Microsoft Forms Name Easily (2026)
metaDescription: Learn how to rename a Microsoft Forms survey or quiz by editing the title directly inside the form editor without breaking your share link.
intro: Fixing a typo or updating a survey title in Microsoft Forms isn't done from the home dashboard grid, since the rename option lives inside the actual form editor. This guide shows how to open your form, click directly into the title field, and update it without breaking your existing share link or losing collected responses. It's a quick fix for anyone managing multiple active questionnaires.

steps.heading: Open your form from the dashboard
steps.body: Locate your target survey tile in the home dashboard and click directly on it to open the full editor view.

steps.heading: Skip the ellipsis menu
steps.body: Note that the three-dot menu on the dashboard grid only handles copying and moving forms, not renaming.

steps.heading: Click the title field
steps.body: At the top of the editor canvas, click directly on the existing header title text to activate an editable cursor.

steps.heading: Type your new title
steps.body: Clear out the old text and type your new descriptive form name.

steps.heading: Confirm the change
steps.body: Press Enter or click elsewhere on the canvas to apply the change, which saves automatically without a dedicated save button.

faq.question: Can I rename a form from the home dashboard grid?
faq.answer: No, the three-dot ellipsis menu on the home grid only handles copying and moving; you need to open the form itself to rename it.

faq.question: Will renaming my form break the existing share link?
faq.answer: No, the backend keeps your existing share URL valid regardless of any title character changes you make.

faq.question: Do I need to click a save button after renaming?
faq.answer: No, Microsoft Forms saves your updated title automatically as soon as you click away from the text field.

faq.question: How do I check how my new title looks to respondents?
faq.answer: Click the Preview button at the top of the editor to see how the updated title renders for someone filling out the form.
